entityName: Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai

metadata.entityName: Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai

ingredients.name: Nishiki In front of the station
Fujisawa Station
ingredients.entityContext: Fujisawa Station is the main transit hub in the series, appearing repeatedly as the starting point for Sakuta and other characters' daily commutes and outings throughout the Shonan arc.

ingredients.name: Shichirigahama Station
ingredients.entityContext: The Enoden station nearest to the school model for Minegahara High School, shown repeatedly in commute scenes where Sakuta and Mai wait for the train or walk toward the beach. Young Shouko first meets Sakuta on the Shichirigahama coast close to this station.

ingredients.name: Kinchoen Sohonke Ogaki Station Main Store
ingredients.localName: 金蝶園総本家 大垣駅前本店

ingredients.name: Katase-Enoshima Station
ingredients.entityContext: The Odakyu Katase-Enoshima Station appears in episode 9, where characters disperse after an all-night gathering at Kugenuma Beach. The distinctive dragon-themed station building was a recognizable landmark for fans.

ingredients.name: Kamakurakoko-Mae Station
ingredients.entityContext: Featured in episodes 9-10 during the Nodoka arc, when Mai's younger sister (who has swapped bodies with Mai) films a commercial at the iconic Enoden crosswalk overlooking the Shonan sea. The crossing is one of the most photographed spots on the entire pilgrimage route.

ingredients.name: Fujisawa Station North Exit Grass Square
ingredients.localName: 藤沢駅北口 芝生広場
ingredients.entityContext: The grassy plaza outside Fujisawa Station's north exit is part of the animated streetscape surrounding the station, appearing in background shots as characters move between the station and the nearby shopping area.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Shrine - Hetsumiya
ingredients.entityContext: The main hall of Enoshima Shrine appears during the Enoshima date scenes, with the torii gate and stone steps serving as recognizable backdrops in episodes featuring Sakuta and Tomoe exploring the island in episode 6.

ingredients.name: Fujisawa City Main Library
ingredients.localName: 藤沢市総合市民図書館
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic opening location of the series: Sakuta Azusagawa first encounters Mai Sakurajima in episode 1 here, dressed in a bunny girl outfit to test whether people can see her due to her adolescence syndrome. This library scene is the defining image of the entire franchise.

ingredients.name: Confort Fujisawa C-2
ingredients.entityContext: This apartment complex in the Fujigaoka neighborhood is identified by pilgrimage guides as one of the architectural models for Sakuta's family residence, visible in scenes depicting the path between the station and his home.

ingredients.name: Karaoke Best 10 Fujisawa
ingredients.localName: カラオケベスト10 藤沢店

ingredients.name: Ueno Zoological Gardens
ingredients.entityContext: Central to Kaede's character arc in episodes 11-13: Kaede's handwritten goal list includes visiting Ueno Zoo to see the pandas, and Sakuta accompanies her there in one of the series' most emotionally resonant scenes before she regains her original memories.

ingredients.name: Katase Nishihama beaches
ingredients.localName: 片瀬西浜・鵠沼海水浴場
ingredients.entityContext: Featured prominently in the opening and ending sequences of the anime, the long sandy stretch near Katase-Enoshima is used for atmospheric beach scenes with the main cast, evoking the Shonan summer mood that defines the series.

ingredients.name: Near the One Story Craftsman House
Honkugenuma Station
ingredients.entityContext: A small Odakyu Enoshima Line station in the Fujisawa beach area, appearing in background shots along the coastal commute route used by characters traveling between the beach zones and Fujisawa Station.

ingredients.name: Bic Camera Fujisawa store
ingredients.localName: ビックカメラ 藤沢店

ingredients.name: Enoshima Aquarium
ingredients.entityContext: The Shin-Enoshima Aquarium (Enosui) appears during the arc involving adolescence syndrome: Mai visits it alone, and it is also the location of Sakuta and Tomoe Koga's date in the Laplace's Demon arc, with the jellyfish tanks providing a memorable backdrop.

ingredients.name: Tobiccho
ingredients.localName: しらす問屋とびっちょ 江の島弁財天仲見世通り店
ingredients.entityContext: The famous shirasu (whitebait) restaurant on Benzaiten Nakamise shopping street is part of the Enoshima area shown during the island scenes, where the pilgrimage route takes fans through the same historic approach road walked by the characters.

ingredients.name: Benzaiten child stone statue
ingredients.entityContext: The stone child deity statues at Enoshima Shrine's Hetsumiya compound are visible in the anime's Enoshima shrine scenes and appear in multiple pilgrimage guides as confirmed background art references.

ingredients.name: Near the Ocean Shop
ingredients.localName: オッターショップ付近

ingredients.name: Mizuho Bank Fujisawa Branch
ingredients.localName: みずほ銀行 藤沢支店

ingredients.name: Seven-Eleven Fujisawa Asahicho Store
ingredients.localName: セブンイレブン 藤沢朝日町店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア

ingredients.name: Lawson Fujisawa-Yanagidori
ingredients.localName: ローソン 藤沢柳通り店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア

ingredients.name: FamilyMart; Asty Ogaki
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート アスティ大垣店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア

ingredients.name: Fujisawa Police Station Fujisawaekikitaguchi Police Box
ingredients.localName: 藤沢警察署 藤沢駅北口交番

ingredients.name: Observatory Square Wood Deck
ingredients.localName: 展望台広場ウッドデッキ
ingredients.entityContext: The outdoor observation deck on Enoshima's upper plateau appears in the series' Enoshima scenes, offering sweeping views of Sagami Bay. The wooden deck area near the Sea Candle serves as a scenic backdrop in moments of reflection.

ingredients.name: Ogaki Station
ingredients.entityContext: In episode 2, Sakuta accompanies Mai on an overnight trip to Ogaki in Gifu Prefecture, hoping to find a place untouched by her adolescence syndrome where people still remember her. The station and surrounding city are central to the episode's emotional test of Mai's worsening condition.

ingredients.name: HAPPY G・FUJISAWA
ingredients.localName: ハッピージーフジサワ
ingredients.address: サンパールビルⅡ 2階・3階, 556 Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0052, Japan

ingredients.name: Anniversaire Cafe
ingredients.localName: アニヴェルセルカフェ みなとみらい横浜
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1-4 Shinko, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In episode 6, this waterfront cafe in Yokohama's Minato Mirai district appears as the location where Sakuta and Tomoe Koga have tea during their fabricated date, with the canal terrace view matching the anime background art.

ingredients.name: Seven-Eleven Fujisawa Station North Exit
ingredients.localName: セブン-イレブン 藤沢駅北口店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア

ingredients.name: Yukiai Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: This small bridge along the route from Shichirigahama Station toward the school model appears in commute scenes, recognized by pilgrimage guides as part of the path Sakuta walks to and from school.

ingredients.name: Utatsuyama Park
ingredients.entityContext: In episode 13's Kanazawa sequence, Sakuta and Mai are taken by her manager to a hilltop viewpoint for a brief late-night date overlooking the city. The Bokotai observatory area of Utatsuyama Park matches the wooden deck and cityscape shown in the episode.

ingredients.name: Katase Higashihama Beach
ingredients.entityContext: The east beach at Katase-Enoshima is part of the broader Shonan coastline shown throughout the series, used for atmospheric seaside scenes and background art that establishes the anime's signature coastal mood.

ingredients.name: Denny's Fujisawa North Exit
ingredients.localName: デニーズ 藤沢北口店
ingredients.type: ファミリー レストラン
categories: ファミリー レストラン
ingredients.entityContext: The model for 'Benny's,' the family restaurant near Fujisawa Station where Sakuta and Tomoe Koga work part-time jobs. Benny's appears in multiple episodes of the Laplace's Demon arc as the setting for key conversations between the two characters.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Glass HOOK Benzaiten Nakamise Street Store
ingredients.localName: 江の島ガラスHOOK 弁財天仲見世通り店
ingredients.entityContext: A shop on the Benzaiten Nakamise shopping street that pilgrimage guides confirm as background art reference material for the Enoshima approach road scenes shown in the series.

ingredients.name: Shichirigahama
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒248-0026 Kanagawa, Kamakura, Shichirigahamahigashi, 2-chōme−1, 七里ガ浜
ingredients.entityContext: Shichirigahama beach is the emotional heart of the series. Sakuta first meets the young version of Shouko Makinohara here, and the open shoreline serves as the backdrop for many pivotal scenes involving Mai, Shouko, and Kaede across the TV series and films.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Sea Candle
ingredients.localName: 江の島シーキャンドル
ingredients.entityContext: The Sea Candle lighthouse tower is a visual anchor for the Enoshima scenes in the series, visible in background art establishing shots whenever characters are on the island. The illuminated tower at night is one of the most recognizable images associated with the anime.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Benten Bridge
ingredients.address: Enoshima Benten Bridge, Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: A recurring symbolic location throughout the entire Rascal Does Not Dream series: the Benten Bridge connecting the mainland to Enoshima Island appears as a turning point location associated with Shouko Makinohara, and is featured prominently in the theatrical film where it becomes a meeting point carrying major plot weight.

ingredients.name: Near the Kuramae 34
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店

ingredients.name: Taisho Bridge
ingredients.description: Taisho Bridge (大正橋) — local bridge in Kamakura, near Daibutsu Kiridoshi Pass and Daikamei Tunnel. Not listed on Google Maps, but the coordinates fall in this area — a verified scene location. Navigate by coordinates / Street View.

ingredients.name: @Park Fujisawa Asahicho
ingredients.localName: アットパーク藤沢朝日町

ingredients.name: Ema Rack
ingredients.entityContext: The 'musubi no ki' ema hanging tree at Enoshima Shrine's Hetsumiya is visible in the shrine compound scenes depicted in the anime, and is a stop on the official pilgrimage route through Enoshima Island.

ingredients.name: Kugenuma-Kaigan Station
ingredients.entityContext: A small Odakyu Enoshima Line station near the Kugenuma beach area, referenced in connection with the episode 8 all-night gathering at Kugenuma Beach that precedes the Katase-Enoshima Station dispersal scene in episode 9.

ingredients.name: Kanazawa Station
ingredients.entityContext: In episode 13, the Kanazawa scenes begin with Sakuta arriving at this station, where Mai's manager arranges for the two to spend a brief evening together before Sakuta must return to Fujisawa. The iconic Tsuzumimon drum gate is a background landmark.

ingredients.name: Haiburitto
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒540-0026 Osaka, Chuo Ward, Uchihonmachi, 1 Chome−2−14

ingredients.name: Coin lockers outside the ticket gates at JR Fujisawa Station
ingredients.localName: JR藤沢駅 改札外コインロッカー

ingredients.name: Near the Union Monthly Fujisawa 2
ingredients.localName: ユニオンマンスリー藤沢２付近
ingredients.type: マンスリー マンション ビル
categories: マンスリー マンション ビル
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒251-0002 Kanagawa, Fujisawa, Daigiri, 1 Chome−4−28 興和ガーデンヒルズ
